articles.headline: The Most Exciting Development in GenUI: Buttons and Checkboxes
articles.deck: AI chats generate simple UI elements to gather context, reduce typing and memory load, and produce more useful, personalized results.

articles.headline: Statistical Significance Isn’t the Same as Practical Significance
articles.deck: Statistical significance helps establish whether a result is reliable, while practical significance helps determine whether it is worth acting on.

articles.headline: UX Conference July Announced (Jul 6 - Jul 14)
articles.deck: Take up to 3 in-depth training courses, teaching user experience best practices for successful design. Live online training focused on long-lasting skills for UX professionals. July 6 - July 14, 2026

articles.headline: GenAI for Complex Questions, Search for Critical Facts
articles.deck: Users choose AI to explore and synthesize information; but they rely on traditional search when accuracy and trust are critical.

articles.headline: Project Postmortems for UX Teams: Learning from Success and Failure
articles.deck: Although postmortems are one of the most powerful learning tools in product development, most teams haven't yet discovered how to use them effectively.

articles.headline: Designing Useful Smart Home Notifications
articles.deck: Smart-home notifications should be timely, relevant, specific, and personalized to avoid overwhelming users and causing distrust or disengagement.

articles.headline: Tracking Adoption of Research Recommendations: The Recommendation-Adoption Score
articles.deck: Use the RAS (Recommendation-Adoption Score) to track how much research value reaches your user.

articles.headline: UX Conference May Announced (May 11 - May 22)
articles.deck: Take up to 5 in-depth training courses, teaching user experience best practices for successful design. Training focused on long-lasting skills for UX professionals. May 11 - May 22, 2026.

articles.headline: UX Research with Minors: Consent vs. Assent
articles.deck: When conducting UX research with minors, you must obtain consent from a parent or legal guardian and assent from the minor participant.

articles.headline: What UX Consulting Clients Expect in the Age of AI
articles.deck: Clients still seek strong judgment and critical thinking, research rigor, and respect for real-world and user constraints from UX consultants.

articles.headline: How AI Literacy Shapes GenAI Use
articles.deck: Using generative AI often doesn’t mean using it well. AI literacy requires both prompt fluency and the ability to assess outputs.

articles.headline: Your Design System Needs an Enforcer
articles.deck: Although design systems promise consistency, most still fail without someone actively enforcing the rules and making teams follow them.

articles.headline: AI-Moderated Interviews: If, When, and How to Use Them
articles.deck: AI interviews offer faster feedback at scale, but they're not a replacement for in-depth, human-led semistructured interviews.

articles.headline: Demand Accuracy in Your AI Tools: Lessons from Baymard Institute
articles.deck: Most AI-powered tools for UX lack reliability and accountability in their outputs. Demand transparency and proven accuracy, or don't buy it.

articles.headline: User Panels 101
articles.deck: A well-built internal user panel saves time, reduces costs, and strengthens your organization’s connection to real users.

articles.headline: Why So Many Info Tips Are Bad (and How to Make Them Better)
articles.deck: Information tips can clarify complex UIs, but they should not hide essential information, trigger redundant information, or disrupt the current workflow.
